content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN pairs of integers. The iii-th pair is (Ai,Bi)(A_i, B_i)(Ai‚Äã,Bi‚Äã).

You also have two piles with you, both initially empty.

You will perform the following process:


For each iii from 111 to NNN, choose **exactly one** of AiA_iAi‚Äã or BiB_iBi‚Äã.
Then, add the chosen integer to one of your two piles.
At the end of the process, both piles **must be non-empty** (i.e, you should have added at least one integer to both).

The value of a pile is defined to be its maximum element.

If you make your choices optimally, find the **minimum possible difference** between the values of the piles.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚Äî the number of pairs.
The next NNN lines describe the pairs. The iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers AiA_iAi‚Äã and BiB_iBi‚Äã ‚Äî the elements of the iii-th pair.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum possible difference between the values of the piles.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The pairs are (1,6)(1, 6)(1,6) and (4,9)(4, 9)(4,9).
Choose 666 from the first pair, and 444 from the second pair, and place them on different piles.
The maximums are 666 and 444, and the difference between them is ‚à£6‚àí4‚à£=2|6-4| = 2‚à£6‚àí4‚à£=2.
Achieving a smaller difference is not possible.
Test case 222: The pairs are (1,9)(1, 9)(1,9) and (4,6)(4, 6)(4,6).
Choose 111 from the first pair and 444 from the second pair and place them on different piles, for a difference of 333.
Test case 333: One optimal solution is:

From the first pair, place 101010 on pile 111.
From the second pair, place 555555 on pile 222.
From the third pair, place 808080 on pile 222.
From the fourth pair, place 919191 on pile 111.

The difference between the maximums of the piles is ‚à£80‚àí91‚à£=11|80 - 91| = 11‚à£80‚àí91‚à£=11. This is the smallest possible difference.
